body.title: Audit: which closed designs have unverified implementation?
body.result: Found 12 open decisions with unverified implementations. 6 NO_CONSEQUENCE gaps: filed T-consequence-igor-web-reply (HIGH), T-consequence-shared-memory-svc (HIGH), T-consequence-dsimnel-parity (MED), T-consequence-or-tiered-cascade (MED), T-consequence-minion-model-upgrade (MED). 6 CONSEQUENCE_OPEN gaps already have consequence tickets in dependency/sprint state. D-web-ui-controls already handled by T-consequence-web-ui-controls.
body.intention: I intend that we identify all designs with closed implementation tickets but no consequence-check completion, so we can triage verification work.
body.description:
**Symptom:** D-web-ui-controls spawned two tickets (both closed 2026-06-04) but the intended four-pane layout never shipped. No consequence-check ticket was filed. Similar pattern likely exists in other designs.

**Task:** Scan all design decisions. For each with status=open or status=closed, check:
1. Are all implementation tickets (non-consequence) status=closed?
2. Is there a consequence-check ticket? If yes, is it also closed?
3. If all impl closed but no consequence or consequence not closed: this is a gap.

For each gap found:
- Decision name, spawned tickets, gap type
- Brief hypothesis: what might not have shipped
- Triage level: HIGH (UI/control visible), MED (internal mechanism), LOW (edge case)

Output format: one finding per line, for filing follow-on tickets per finding.

**Affected files:** lab/design_docs/decisions/*.md (read-only); no code changes
**Design rules:** none apply
**Scope boundary:** IN: identify gaps, triage level, brief reason. OUT: fix any gaps (separate tickets per finding).
**Completion criteria:** Report lists all gaps by triage level with decision name, impl tickets, and brief hypothesis. File one follow-on consequence ticket per HIGH/MED finding (file as T-consequence-{design-slug}).
**Test plan:** Manual inspection of findings. Spot-check 3 HIGH findings via grep/code review to confirm gap is real.
